goo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Gone Dau.
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
got: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gothic.
gout: noun. An extremely painful inflammation of joints, especially of the big toe, caused by a metabolic defect resulting in the accumulation of
grout: noun. A thin mortar used to fill the gaps between tiles and cavities in masonry.
gut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Maléku Jaíka.
hog: noun. Any animal belonging to the Suidae family of mammals, especially the pig, the warthog, and the boar.
hoot: noun. A derisive cry or shout.
hot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Hote.
hour: noun. A unit of time of one twenty-fourth of a day (sixty minutes).
hut: noun. A small, simple one- storey dwelling or shelter, often with just one room, and generally built of readily available local materials.
oho: interjection. Expressing surprise or gloating realisation; aha.
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
ortho: noun. An isomer of a benzene derivative having two substituents adjacent on the ring.
rho: noun. The seventeenth letter of the Modern Greek and Classical alphabets and the nineteenth letter of Old and Ancient.
root: noun. The part of a plant, generally underground, that anchors and supports the plant body, absorbs and stores water and nutrients, and in
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rout: noun. A group of people; a crowd, a throng, a troop; in particular, a group of people accompanying or travelling with someone.
rug: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Roviana.
rut: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Rutul.
ruth: noun. Sorrow for the misery of another person; compassion, mercy, pity.
thorough: adjective. Painstaking and careful not to miss or omit any detail.
through: preposition. From one side or end of (something) to the other.
thug: noun. A person who is a member of a gang or criminal organization.
tog: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tonga (Malawi).
too: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Xicotepec de Juárez Totonac.
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
trough: noun. A long, narrow container, open on top, for feeding or watering animals.
trug: noun. A shallow, oval basket used for gardening
ugh: interjection. Used to express repugnance, disgust, or annoyance.
